description_long: This object is a vintage gramophone-style record player featuring a large, flared brass horn that dominates its upper structure, curving outward from a narrow neck to a wide, circular opening. The horn is mounted on a dark, rectangular wooden base that houses the turntable and mechanical components. A small, round vinyl record is visible on the platter, with a slender tonearm extending over it, suggesting functionality. The base includes a hand-crank mechanism on one side, typical of early mechanical record players, and the overall design is ornate and classic, evoking early 20th-century aesthetics. The materials appear to be primarily polished brass for the horn and dark-stained wood for the base, with some metallic and plastic elements for the tonearm and crank. The object is compact, likely intended for tabletop use, and would serve as a decorative or functional piece in an urban vintage shop, café, or home.
description: A vintage gramophone-style record player with a large brass horn, dark wooden base, visible vinyl record, and hand-crank mechanism.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, circular opening of the brass horn and the rectangular wooden base beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length and curvature of the brass horn, the tonearm over the record, and the hand-crank on the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the narrow end of the horn, the back of the wooden base, and the crank handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the horn's profile, the tonearm, and the record on the platter.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and proportionally small compared to the large horn, located centrally on the wooden base. It appears to be made of black plastic or metal, with a visible vinyl record placed on top. The platter has a smooth, glossy finish and no visible grooves or patterns beyond the record itself.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, slender, and metallic, extending from the side of the base over the record. It is medium in length, slightly curved, and appears to be made of metal with a polished finish. The tonearm is positioned as if ready to play the record.
